CLINICALLY RELEVANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

TIER I вЂ” GENOMIC ALTERATIONS WITH STRONG CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE
(FDA-approved therapy or inclusion criteria for clinical trials directly supported by evidence)

  Gene       Alteration                 Exon   Type         Allele Freq   Classification
  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  EGFR       p.L858R (c.2573T>G)        21     Missense     38.2%         PATHOGENIC вЂ” Activating

Interpretation:
EGFR p.L858R is a well-characterized activating point mutation in exon 21 of the epidermal growth factor receptor gene, resulting in constitutive activation of downstream RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT signaling. This mutation is detected in approximately 40вЂ“45% of EGFR-mutant non-small cell lung cancers in East Asian populations and approximately 25вЂ“30% in Western populations. The allele frequency of 38.2% is consistent with the estimated tumor purity and is consistent with a clonal, founding mutation. EGFR exon 21 L858R is an established predictive biomarker for response to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs) including erlotinib, gefitinib, afatinib, dacomitinib, and osimertinib. Per current guidelines (NCCN, ESMO, IASLC), patients with EGFR-activating mutations should be offered first-line EGFR TKI therapy; osimertinib (Tagrisso) demonstrates superior progression-free and overall survival compared with first- and second-generation TKIs in this mutation context (FLAURA trial). This mutation is a companion diagnostic (CDx) biomarker per FDA-approved labeling.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
ADDITIONAL GENOMIC ALTERATIONS вЂ” CO-MUTATIONS AND VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

  Gene       Alteration                     Exon   Type         Allele Freq   Classification
  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  TP53       p.R248W (c.742C>T)             7      Missense     42.1%         PATHOGENIC вЂ” Loss-of-function
  SMARCA4    p.R1192H (c.3575G>A)           25     Missense     14.8%         VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE
  CDH1       p.E243K (c.727G>A)             6      Missense     9.3%          VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE

TP53 p.R248W:
TP53 p.R248W is one of the most frequently observed gain-of-function/dominant-negative TP53 missense mutations in human cancer, occurring at the critical DNA-contact residue arginine 248 within the DNA-binding domain. This mutation abolishes wild-type tumor suppressor function and confers oncogenic gain-of-function properties, promoting genomic instability, resistance to apoptosis, and dysregulated cell cycle progression. In EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, co-occurring TP53 mutations are detected in approximately 40вЂ“50% of cases and have been associated with somewhat shorter progression-free survival on first-generation EGFR TKIs in retrospective analyses, though data for osimertinib in this context are limited. The allele frequency of 42.1% вЂ” slightly higher than the EGFR L858R allele frequency вЂ” is consistent with TP53 mutation occurring early in tumor evolution (possibly in a pre-malignant clone) or in a slightly more prevalent subclone. There are no approved therapies directly targeting TP53 p.R248W at this time, though several investigational approaches (including APR-246/eprenetapopt) are in clinical development for TP53-mutant malignancies. This co-mutation should be noted for its potential prognostic relevance and as a baseline comparator for future molecular profiling at disease progression.

SMARCA4 p.R1192H:
SMARCA4 encodes BRG1, the catalytic ATPase subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin-remodeling complex. Pathogenic loss-of-function mutations in SMARCA4 define a distinct subset of NSCLC (SMARCA4-deficient thoracic sarcomatoid carcinoma), but this variant at p.R1192H has not been functionally characterized and is reported as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS) in available databases (ClinVar, COSMIC). The allele frequency of 14.8% suggests this may represent a subclonal alteration. Its functional impact on SWI/SNF activity or EGFR TKI sensitivity is unknown. No approved targeted therapies apply.

CDH1 p.E243K:
CDH1 encodes E-cadherin, a transmembrane glycoprotein central to epithelial cellвЂ“cell adhesion. Pathogenic CDH1 mutations are associated with hereditary diffuse gastric cancer (HDGC) and lobular breast cancer, though somatic CDH1 alterations are observed in lung adenocarcinoma at low frequency. The p.E243K variant has not been definitively classified as pathogenic in lung cancer and is reported as a VUS. The allele frequency of 9.3% suggests a subclonal occurrence. No clinical actionability in the lung cancer context at this time. Given the patient's personal and family cancer history, germline implications of CDH1 p.E243K should be considered in the context of genetic counseling if not already performed, though somatic-only status must be evaluated.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

  Gene       Alteration               Copy Number Ratio   Interpretation
  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  MET        Amplification (low-level)    2.4             LOW-LEVEL GAIN вЂ” Not meeting threshold for high-level MET amplification (threshold: в‰Ґ5.0 or MET/CEP7 ratio в‰Ґ2.2 by FISH with в‰Ґ4 copies). Subthreshold; likely not predictive of MET inhibitor response at this level. Recommend clinical correlation and serial molecular monitoring.
  CCND1      Gain                         3.1             LOW-LEVEL GAIN вЂ” CCND1 (cyclin D1, chromosome 11q13) gain is common in lung adenocarcinoma and may reflect chromosome 11 polysomy. Functional significance uncertain in this context.

Note on MET amplification: Low-level MET copy number gain (ratio 2.4) is detected and falls below the established threshold for clinically actionable MET amplification in NSCLC (typically defined as copy number ratio в‰Ґ5 or high polysomy/gene amplification by FISH). This may reflect true low-level amplification or polysomy, and does not currently meet criteria for MET exon 14 skip-independent MET amplification-directed therapy. FISH confirmatory testing may be considered if clinically indicated. This finding should be monitored on serial profiling, particularly in the context of acquired resistance, as MET amplification is a well-recognized acquired resistance mechanism following EGFR TKI therapy.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
RELEVANT NEGATIVE FINDINGS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
The following genomic alterations were specifically evaluated and were NOT detected:

  Alteration                                    Result
  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ  в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  EGFR exon 19 deletion (del E746-A750, etc.)   NOT DETECTED
  EGFR T790M (exon 20)                          NOT DETECTED
  EGFR exon 20 insertion                        NOT DETECTED
  EGFR G719X (exon 18)                          NOT DETECTED
  EGFR S768I (exon 20)                          NOT DETECTED
  EGFR L861Q (exon 21)                          NOT DETECTED
  KRAS (all codons, including G12/G13/Q61)      NOT DETECTED
  NRAS mutation                                 NOT DETECTED
  BRAF V600E                                    NOT DETECTED
  BRAF non-V600 mutations                       NOT DETECTED
  ALK fusion (all partners)                     NOT DETECTED
  ROS1 fusion (all partners)                    NOT DETECTED
  RET fusion (all partners)                     NOT DETECTED
  MET exon 14 skipping mutation                 NOT DETECTED
  NTRK1 fusion                                  NOT DETECTED
  NTRK2 fusion                                  NOT DETECTED
  NTRK3 fusion                                  NOT DETECTED
  ERBB2 (HER2) amplification                   NOT DETECTED
  ERBB2 (HER2) exon 20 insertion               NOT DETECTED
  ERBB2 (HER2) point mutation                  NOT DETECTED
  PIK3CA mutation (all hotspots)               NOT DETECTED
  PTEN loss/mutation                            NOT DETECTED
  STK11 mutation                                NOT DETECTED
  KEAP1 mutation                                NOT DETECTED
  NF1 mutation                                  NOT DETECTED
  EGFR C797S                                    NOT DETECTED
  Homologous recombination deficiency (HRD)     NOT DETECTED

Note: The absence of KRAS mutation in the setting of an EGFR activating mutation is expected, as EGFR and KRAS mutations are mutually exclusive in lung adenocarcinoma in the vast majority of cases, reflecting distinct oncogenic driver biology. The absence of STK11 and KEAP1 mutations is clinically relevant; co-occurring STK11 or KEAP1 mutations in EGFR-mutant NSCLC have been associated with resistance to immunotherapy and may impact treatment sequencing decisions.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
GENE FUSION / REARRANGEMENT PANEL
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
RNA-based fusion analysis was not performed on this specimen due to specimen quality constraints; however, DNA-based fusion detection for ALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK1/2/3, and MET exon 14 skip was performed via hybrid capture sequencing. All fusion gene breakpoints evaluated вЂ” NOT DETECTED.

Note: DNA-based fusion detection has sensitivity limitations for certain rare fusion partners. In the setting of a confirmed EGFR L858R driver mutation, the clinical probability of a co-occurring oncogenic fusion is extremely low; however, if clinically indicated (e.g., at disease progression without clear molecular resistance mechanism), RNA-based fusion analysis may be considered on subsequent biopsy material.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
IMMUNOTHERAPY BIOMARKERS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):   4 mutations/Mb  вЂ”  LOW
  (TMB-High defined as в‰Ґ10 mutations/Mb per FDA companion diagnostic threshold)
Microsatellite Status (MSI/MSS): MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
  (Evaluated across 95 intronic homopolymer and dinucleotide repeat loci)

Interpretation: This tumor does not meet biomarker criteria for pembrolizumab (Keytruda) based on TMB-High (в‰Ґ10 mut/Mb) or MSI-H. TMB of 4 mut/Mb is in the low range for NSCLC. Additionally, in the clinical context of an EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, the benefit of PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitor therapy is substantially attenuated compared with EGFR wild-type NSCLC. Multiple clinical trials (IMpower150 subset analyses, KEYNOTE-789) have demonstrated limited single-agent immunotherapy activity in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, and concurrent EGFR TKI plus checkpoint inhibitor combinations are associated with excess pneumonitis toxicity. PD-L1 TPS of 5% (by 22C3 IHC, performed separately) is in the low-positive range and further supports that immunotherapy is unlikely to provide meaningful clinical benefit in the first-line setting for this patient.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
GERMLINE / HEREDITARY CONSIDERATIONS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
FoundationOne CDx performs tumor-only sequencing on FFPE tissue and does not include a paired germline control. Variants detected by tumor-only profiling may represent somatic tumor mutations, germline variants, or clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP). The following observations are noted:

- CDH1 p.E243K (VUS, AF 9.3%): CDH1 germline pathogenic variants are associated with hereditary diffuse gastric cancer syndrome and lobular breast cancer predisposition. Although p.E243K is currently classified as a VUS and the allele frequency of 9.3% does not by itself suggest a germline origin (germline variants are typically present at ~50% AF in tumor-only sequencing if heterozygous), the finding in the context of a family history of cancer warrants consideration of germline genetic counseling referral to formally evaluate hereditary cancer risk, particularly if the patient has not previously undergone germline testing.

- TP53 p.R248W (AF 42.1%): Allele frequency compatible with either somatic clonal mutation or possible germline (Li-Fraumeni syndrome) context. Given patient age at lung cancer diagnosis (63 years) and no reported history of early-onset malignancies, a germline TP53 origin is considered low probability but cannot be excluded without paired germline sequencing. Referral for genetic counseling is reasonable if clinically indicated.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
SUMMARY OF FINDINGS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
This comprehensive genomic profile of a right upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma in a 63-year-old East Asian female with a remote smoking history reveals:

1. EGFR p.L858R (exon 21) вЂ” Clonal, activating, pathogenic driver mutation at allele frequency 38.2%. This is the primary oncogenic driver and a companion diagnostic biomarker for EGFR TKI therapy (erlotinib, gefitinib, afatinib, dacomitinib, osimertinib). Osimertinib (Tagrisso, third-generation, CNS-penetrant EGFR TKI) is the preferred first-line agent per NCCN guidelines and FLAURA trial data.

2. TP53 p.R248W (exon 7) вЂ” Co-occurring pathogenic TP53 gain-of-function mutation at high allele frequency (42.1%), likely an early clonal event. No direct therapeutic actionability; prognostic relevance as a co-mutation in EGFR-mutant NSCLC should be noted.

3. SMARCA4 p.R1192H (exon 25) вЂ” VUS, subclonal (AF 14.8%). No actionability.

4. CDH1 p.E243K (exon 6) вЂ” VUS, subclonal (AF 9.3%). Germline implications uncertain; genetic counseling referral may be considered.

5. MET low-level copy number gain (ratio 2.4) вЂ” Below threshold for actionable MET amplification. Serial monitoring at progression recommended given MET amplification as a known EGFR TKI acquired resistance mechanism.

6. CCND1 gain вЂ” Low-level, no direct actionability.

7. TMB Low (4 mut/Mb), MSS вЂ” Does not support checkpoint inhibitor use based on these biomarkers.

8. No EGFR T790M, no EGFR exon 19 deletion, no KRAS mutation, no ALK/ROS1/RET/NTRK fusion, no HER2 amplification/mutation, no MET exon 14 skip, no STK11/KEAP1/NF1 mutations detected.

METHODOLOGY
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
FoundationOne CDx (F1CDx) is a next-generation sequencing-based in vitro diagnostic device (IVD) approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (PMA P170019). The assay profiles 324 cancer-related genes for substitutions, insertions/deletions, copy number alterations, and select gene rearrangements/fusions. The assay is performed on FFPE tissue using hybrid capture-based enrichment followed by massively parallel paired-end sequencing on an Illumina HiSeq platform. Minimum tumor content: 20% (pathologist-estimated). Minimum DNA input: 50 ng. TMB is calculated from somatic synonymous and non-synonymous mutations across the sequenced region (approximately 1.1 Mb). MSI is assessed across 95 intronic repeat loci. Sequencing depth: >500x mean coverage across targeted regions. Sensitivity for substitutions and small indels: >99% at tumor fraction в‰Ґ20%. This assay is a companion diagnostic for multiple FDA-approved therapies including osimertinib (Tagrisso) for EGFR exon 19 deletions and exon 21 L858R substitutions, erlotinib (Tarceva), gefitinib (Iressa), afatinib (Gilotrif), dacomitinib (Vizimpro), and pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for TMB-High (в‰Ґ10 mut/Mb) solid tumors.
